title: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq 100 Slips As Broadcom Plunges, Bitcoin Hits 4-Month Lows
author: Piero Cingari
description: The Nasdaq 100 fell 0.7% as chip stocks stumbled, with Broadcom cratering 15% despite beating earnings due to weak AI-chip revenue guidance. The Dow Jones rallied 1.7% on defensive and financial strength, while the S&P 500 remained flat. Bitcoin hit a 4-month low at $61,310. Other tech stocks like CrowdStrike, Ciena, and Five Below also declined despite beating estimates, reflecting a 'beat-and-bleed' market dynamic. Healthcare and financial stocks led gains.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock plunged 15% despite beating adjusted earnings estimates ($2.44 EPS) due to disappointing AI-chip revenue outlook that fell short of market expectations.
Fell 7.2% after post-close earnings report, exemplifying the 'beat-and-bleed' market dynamic where strong results fail to impress investors.
Sank 18.4% despite beating EPS estimates ($1.64 vs expected), posting 40% YoY revenue growth, and raising full-year guidance to $6.3B, indicating investor skepticism.
Dropped 13.3% despite posting strong adjusted EPS ($2.22 vs $1.69 estimate) and lifting annual forecast, as investors questioned guidance durability given tariff assumptions.
Plunged 20.9% (worst Russell 1000 performer) after beating Q1 earnings but cutting full-year revenue outlook to flat due to Middle East headwinds and tariff effects.
Rose 7.8%, recovering from prior session's slump as financial stocks rallied on defensive leadership and alternative-asset strength.
Climbed 7.1% in sympathy with broader financial and alternative-asset sector rally.
Jumped 11.7% as healthcare-AI names surged, benefiting from precision-diagnostics sector strength.
Rose 9.4% alongside other healthcare-AI stocks as precision-diagnostics sector rallied.
Led all sector groups with a 2.8% gain, reflecting strong defensive positioning and healthcare strength.
Up 2.6%, closely trailing healthcare as financial stocks rallied on defensive and alternative-asset leadership.
Sank 2% as chip selloff rippled across the technology sector.
Dropped 2.3% in the worst showing among industry groups due to broad chip sector weakness.
Jumped 2.8% as financial stocks rallied on defensive leadership.
Climbed 2.5% as healthcare sector outperformed.

title: INVESTIGATION NOTICE: Girard Sharp Law Firm Encourages Former Investors of Blue Owl Capital Corp. III (NYSE: OBDE) Who Received Shares of Blue Owl Capital Corp. (NYSE: OBDC), and Former Investors of Blue Owl Technology Finance Corp. II Who Received Shares of Blue Owl Technology Finance Corp. (NYSE: OTF), to Contact the Firm
author: Girard Sharp Llp
description: Girard Sharp LLP is investigating potential securities claims on behalf of former investors in Blue Owl Capital Corp. III and Blue Owl Technology Finance Corp. II who received shares in merger transactions. Blue Owl Capital's shares have declined over 21% since January 2025, while Blue Owl Technology's shares have fallen over 30% since June 2025. The investigation focuses on potential undisclosed issues with the funds' investment portfolios.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Share price declined over 21% since merger completion on January 13, 2025, with investigation into potential undisclosed portfolio issues suggesting investor losses and potential securities violations.

Share price declined over 30% since NYSE listing on June 13, 2025, with investigation into potential undisclosed portfolio issues indicating significant investor losses and potential securities violations.
As the external manager of both affected funds facing investigations, the firm's reputation and potential liability exposure are negatively impacted by the securities investigations.

title: JPMorgan Chase Says It Is Comfortable With $50 Billion in Private Credit Exposure. Should Investors Be?
author: Reuben Gregg Brewer
description: JPMorgan Chase maintains $50 billion in private credit exposure despite concerns in the sector, with competitors BlackRock and Blue Owl limiting withdrawals from private credit funds. CEO Jamie Dimon argues the exposure is manageable given JPMorgan's $821 billion market cap and diversified portfolio, noting the $1.8 trillion private credit market is smaller than mortgage and investment-grade bond markets. While BDC investors should be cautious, JPMorgan's scale and diversification make the exposure less risky.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: JPMorgan's $50 billion private credit exposure is presented as manageable and well-positioned relative to its $821 billion market cap and $1.5 trillion in total loans. The bank's diversification and scale provide adequate cushion against private credit market risks.
